value.found_quote: Antigravity is architected as an agent-first platform, meaning the Agent Manager (Mission Control) automatically delegates specific tasks to specialized Sub-agents to optimize performance and context usage. For example, when a task requires web research or documentation lookup, the system invokes the Browser Subagent; for command-line operations, it uses the Terminal Subagent.
value.rationale: The page (a Google AI Developers Forum thread) describes that Antigravity already has built-in sub-agents (Browser Subagent, Terminal Subagent) delegated by the Agent Manager/Mission Control. While users request user-defined custom sub-agents (not yet available), the offering of Antigravity sub-agents themselves is substantiated as a current, active feature. Note the claim's surrounding marketing text mentions 'AgentKit 2.0' and '16 specialized agents,' which the page does not corroborate; however, the core named offering — Antigravity sub-agents/Agent Manager orchestration — is described on the page.
